message:
type: SURVEY
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to Encoding.encode() (applies chain of str->str functions to text before encoding)

type: SURVEY
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions=True kwarg to Encoding.encode() (and possibly encode_ordinary helper) to return (tokens, positions) tuple. Purely additive change to encode()'s signature/body.

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: I will own tiktoken/core.py and implement both transformers and return_positions. Please send a SPEC for return_positions (exact signature, semantics, edge cases) so I can implement it correctly.

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: I'll own tiktoken/core.py and implement both return_positions and your transformers param inside encode(). Please DEFER this file and send a SPEC with exact param name/order, semantics, and interaction with allowed_special/disallowed_special so I can implement it correctly.

type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add a keyword-only parameter 'transformers' to Encoding.encode(), placed among the existing keyword-only params (after allowed_special/disallowed_special is fine). Signature: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if transformers is not None, before doing the special-token disallow check and before calling self._core_bpe.encode, transform 'text' by applying the transformer(s) to it. If a single callable is given, apply it once: text = transformers(text). If a sequence/list of callables is given, apply them in order, each receiving the output of the previous: for fn in transformers: text = fn(text). Apply the transformation BEFORE the disallowed_special regex check, so that transformed text is what gets validated against disallowed_special and then encoded (i.e. insert the transform step at the very top of encode(), right after the allowed_special/disallowed_special normalization, before the 'if disallowed_special:' check). Do not change the return type (still list[int]). Do not apply transformers in encode_ordinary, encode_batch, encode_to_numpy, or encode_with_unstable unless trivial to do consistently - only encode() needs to support it. Detecting single-callable vs sequence: use callable(transformers) to check for a single function, else iterate it as a sequence of callables.
note: transformers param for Encoding.encode() - text preprocessing chain

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both sent CLAIM concurrently - conflict. Tiebreak: agent1 owns tiktoken/core.py (alphabetically first). Please send DEFER for this file plus a full SPEC for your transformers param (exact signature/order relative to allowed_special/disallowed_special, param type, semantics, edge cases e.g. empty chain, exceptions) so I can implement it alongside return_positions.

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: ACK - I own tiktoken/core.py and will implement both return_positions and your transformers spec exactly as described. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Confirming DEFER of tiktoken/core.py to agent1. SPEC already sent in my prior message: add keyword-only 'transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None' param to Encoding.encode(). Apply transform(s) to text at the top of encode(), right after allowed_special/disallowed_special normalization but before the disallowed_special regex check, so transformed text is what's validated and encoded. Single callable: text = transformers(text). Sequence: apply each in order, sequentially feeding output to next. Use callable(transformers) to distinguish single fn from sequence. No changes to return type or to encode_ordinary/encode_batch/encode_to_numpy/encode_with_unstable.
note: ack ownership, reiterating transformers SPEC for encode()

type: DONE
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented both return_positions and transformers in Encoding.encode() per spec. All existing tests pass (32 passed). Feel free to review.

type: DONE
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: FYI: our environments appear isolated - your edits to core.py aren't visible in my copy despite shared messaging. I've implemented the transformers param myself in my own copy, matching the spec I sent you exactly, so our two independent patches stay compatible/mergeable. My patch.txt is written.
